text:
Market today: Geopolitical tensions from Hormuz and Iran strikes are creating market volatility, but the chip sector got a boost from ASML's strong AI chip demand forecast. This creates a mixed mood for your tech-heavy book.

Your holdings: SOXL (-30.59%) and SPAL (-36.78%) are flagged as SELL/NEW. Both are leveraged ETFs, decay over time, and are showing significant losses. SPAL is brand-new and very volatile; remember margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on geopolitical developments and any further news regarding AI chip demand. These will be key drivers for your concentrated portfolio.

Bottom line: Prioritize managing risk on your leveraged positions while staying alert to opportunities in resilient chip names.
plays: SOXL: Respect your stop at 200. This leveraged ETF is down significantly, and market volatility from geopolitical events could worsen drawdowns.
SPAL: This new, leveraged ETF is down big. Its default stop at 21.38 is critical to respect, especially with margin amplifying losses.
AMD: Consider a new buy if you're looking to add to chips, backed by strong AI demand news and scanner setup. Entry 547.58-547.03, stop 545.95.
